evals.prompt: I want to add semantic search to my Python script. I have a list of 500 product descriptions and I need to find the most relevant ones for a user's query. No OpenAI API — it needs to run on CPU. Show me how to do it.
evals.expected_output: Code using SentenceTransformer('all-MiniLM-L6-v2'), model.encode() with normalize_embeddings=True, and util.semantic_search(). Must NOT use openai, cohere, or any cloud embedding API. Should encode corpus once and reuse embeddings. Top-k results returned as a list with document text and score.
expectations: Uses sentence-transformers library (not openai/cohere/any paid API)
Imports SentenceTransformer and util from sentence_transformers
Uses model.encode() (not model.rank() — that is a CrossEncoder method)
Passes normalize_embeddings=True to model.encode()
Uses util.semantic_search() to retrieve top-k results
Does NOT separately compute cosine similarity after encoding without normalization
Mentions that model downloads from HuggingFace once then runs offline
Returns or prints the matched documents with their scores
Encodes corpus once (not re-encodes on every query)

evals.prompt: My search script uses BM25 but I'm getting really bad recall — relevant documents aren't being found. The code builds the index from lowercase text but the queries go in as-is (mixed case). How do I diagnose and fix this?
evals.expected_output: Identifies the root cause: asymmetric tokenization (corpus lowercased, query not). Fix: apply the exact same tokenization pipeline to both corpus at index time and query at search time. Shows corrected code where tokenize() function is used for both.
expectations: Correctly identifies asymmetric tokenization as the root cause
Explains that BM25 is a term-matching algorithm requiring identical preprocessing
Shows a tokenize() function applied to BOTH corpus items and queries
The fix applies the same lowercase transformation to the query
Does not introduce different stop-word lists for corpus vs query
Shows use of get_scores() or get_top_n() with the fixed tokenized_query
Mentions that recall is expected to improve after fix
Does NOT suggest switching to semantic search as the primary fix (that is a different tool)

evals.prompt: I have a retrieval system and I want to measure its quality. For each query I have a list of ground-truth relevant documents (binary: 0 or 1). I want NDCG@10 and MAP. How do I compute these locally without any external service?
evals.expected_output: Code using scripts/metrics.py (or the equivalent inline implementation). Shows building a relevances list (ground-truth labels in retrieval order), calling ndcg_at_k(relevances, k=10) and mean_average_precision(list_of_relevances). Explains the MAP vs sklearn.average_precision_score difference.
expectations: Provides a working NDCG@k implementation or points to scripts/metrics.py
Explains that relevances list = ground-truth grades in retrieval order (index 0 = rank 1)
Calls ndcg_at_k with k=10
Provides or calls mean_average_precision across multiple queries
Does NOT use sklearn.average_precision_score and claim it equals MAP (different formula)
Either notes or warns about the MAP vs sklearn.average_precision_score distinction
Implementation uses numpy (no sklearn required for the metrics themselves)
For binary labels, mentions that gains='exponential' and gains='linear' give same ranking order

evals.prompt: I want to combine BM25 and semantic search for better retrieval. My corpus has 2,000 technical support articles. Show me the full hybrid search implementation that fuses results from both.
evals.expected_output: Full implementation with: (1) BM25Okapi indexing the corpus with consistent tokenization, (2) SentenceTransformer embedding the corpus, (3) a reciprocal_rank_fusion function using k=60 as the standard constant, (4) a hybrid_search function that runs both retrievers and fuses their ranked doc-ID lists.
expectations: Implements BM25Okapi retrieval using rank_bm25
Implements semantic retrieval using SentenceTransformer and util.semantic_search
Implements Reciprocal Rank Fusion with k=60 (standard constant)
RRF function accepts ranked lists of doc IDs (not scores)
RRF score = sum of 1/(k + rank) across retrievers
Corpus is encoded once and embeddings reused across queries
BM25 tokenization is consistent between corpus indexing and query search
Final output returns doc IDs (or doc text) sorted by fused RRF score
Does NOT use a paid API for embeddings

evals.prompt: Should I use BM25 or semantic search for my use case? I'm building a search over a dataset of error log messages. Users search by typing the exact error code or message text (e.g., 'ERROR_CODE_4021 connection refused').
evals.expected_output: Recommendation of BM25 for this use case with clear rationale: exact-match term retrieval is BM25's strength; error codes and technical identifiers are not well-represented in semantic embedding spaces trained on general text. Hybrid may help but BM25 alone is a strong starting point.
expectations: Recommends BM25 as the primary retriever for exact-match error codes
Explains WHY: BM25 excels at exact term matching; error codes are out-of-vocabulary for most embedding models
Does NOT recommend semantic search alone for this case
Mentions hybrid search (BM25 + semantic) as an option to handle paraphrastic queries alongside exact codes
Gives a concrete example of why semantic search would struggle (e.g., 'ERROR_CODE_4021' has no semantic neighbor in embedding space)
Suggests starting with BM25 and adding semantic search only if recall is insufficient

evals.prompt: I built a retrieval pipeline and ran it against 50 test queries. I have the ground-truth relevant doc IDs for each query. Now I want to add evaluation code that computes NDCG@5, P@5, and Recall@5 for each query and prints a summary. Relevance is graded: 0, 1, 2, or 3.
evals.expected_output: Code that builds per-query relevance lists (grades in retrieval order), calls ndcg_at_k, precision_at_k, and recall_at_k for each query, then computes and prints averages. Uses scripts/metrics.py or equivalent pure-numpy implementation. Chooses gains='exponential' for NDCG (correct for graded relevance).
expectations: Builds relevance list as ground-truth grades in the retrieval-output order
Calls ndcg_at_k(relevances, k=5) — using graded relevance correctly
Calls precision_at_k(relevances, k=5)
Calls recall_at_k(relevances, k=5, num_relevant=...) with the correct total-relevant count
Uses gains='exponential' or default (not 'linear') since labels are graded 0-3
Computes per-query metrics and aggregates (averages) across all 50 queries
Prints a summary with mean NDCG@5, mean P@5, mean Recall@5
Does NOT use sklearn.ndcg_score with wrong array shapes (requires 2D inputs)
Implementation is pure numpy or uses scripts/metrics.py (no sklearn dependency at runtime)

evals.prompt: I need to add semantic search to a script that will run on a server without a GPU. The server has limited RAM (2 GB available for the process). What's the most CPU-efficient approach? The corpus has ~10,000 short sentences.
evals.expected_output: Recommends all-MiniLM-L6-v2 as the default CPU choice (~22 MB model). Mentions static embeddings (model2vec/potion-base-8M) as a faster alternative when latency is critical. Recommends persisting corpus embeddings to disk (numpy save/load) to avoid re-encoding. Notes that 10k×384-dim float32 corpus embeddings = ~15 MB RAM.
expectations: Recommends all-MiniLM-L6-v2 for the CPU use case
Mentions model size (~22 MB) so the user knows RAM impact
Notes that corpus embeddings for 10k docs at 384-dim ≈ 15 MB float32 — fits in 2GB RAM
Recommends caching/persisting embeddings to disk (np.save / np.load) to avoid re-encoding
Mentions static embeddings (model2vec) as a speed alternative when encoding latency is a bottleneck
Does NOT recommend GPU-only solutions or cloud APIs
Shows or mentions normalize_embeddings=True for correct cosine similarity

evals.prompt: What metric should I use to evaluate my retrieval system? My system returns a ranked list of 10 results per query. I have binary relevance labels (a document is either relevant or not). I care most about whether the top results are relevant.
evals.expected_output: Recommends MAP and/or P@k as primary metrics for binary relevance. Explains that NDCG is designed for graded relevance but works for binary (equivalent to MAP in ranking order). Recommends P@3 or P@5 since user cares about top results. Notes that MRR is appropriate if only the single best result matters.
expectations: Recommends MAP or P@k for binary relevance use case
Explains that P@k directly measures top-result quality (aligns with stated concern)
Mentions MRR as appropriate if finding one correct result at the top is the only goal
If recommending NDCG, explains that for binary labels gains='linear' and 'exponential' produce equivalent rankings
Does NOT recommend NDCG@k alone without noting the binary-vs-graded relevance distinction
Gives a concrete recommendation (e.g., 'use P@5 and MAP') rather than listing all metrics without guidance
Does NOT confuse sklearn.average_precision_score (PR-curve AP) with ranking MAP
